title: MUD World State
description: The 8 narrative constants that are generated once at session start and locked into the LearningState for the session's duration. Produced by generate_mud_world() from mud-world-templates.yaml. Education domain only.

template_id.description: The id of the source template from mud-world-templates.yaml. Included for traceability in CommitmentRecord.

zone.description: The physical in-world setting where all math problems take place during this session. The LLM must use this as the environmental backdrop.

protagonist.description: The student's in-world role or title. The LLM addresses the student using this role, not their real name.

antagonist.description: The boss character who taunts the student and creates narrative pressure. Taunt frequency and content are controlled by persona rules in domain-system-override.md.

guide_npc.description: The in-world character who delivers hints. All hint interactions are narrated in this character's voice.

macguffin.description: The objective the protagonist is pursuing. Every equation is framed as a step toward obtaining the macguffin.

variable_skin.description: The in-world term for the unknown variable(s). The LLM must NEVER use a bare variable letter (e.g. 'x') — it must use this label instead. The underlying algebra is unchanged.

obstacle_theme.description: How equations are physically represented in this world (e.g. 'Magical Counter-Weight Scales', 'Encrypted Firewalls'). Used to narrate the student's physical actions.

failure_state.description: The exact narrative consequence when an invariant is violated. Used VERBATIM by the orchestrator — not paraphrased. Keeps mistakes feeling like game mechanics rather than test red-marks.
